cases.description: Ensures that clients can correctly parse datetime timestamps with fractional seconds
Ensures that operations with errors successfully know how to deserialize the successful response

given.name: GreetingWithErrors
given.documentation:
This operation has three possible return values:

  1. A successful response in the form of GreetingWithErrorsOutput
  2. An InvalidGreeting error.
  3. A BadRequest error.

Implementations must be able to successfully take a response and properly (de)serialize successful and error responses based on the the presence of the

InvalidGreeting.documentation: This error is thrown when an invalid greeting value is provided.

Implementations must be able to successfully take a response and properly (de)serialize successful and error responses based on the the presence of the

ComplexError.documentation: This error is thrown when a request is invalid.

cases.description: Serializes simple scalar properties

Serializes string with escaping.

This validates the three escape types: literal, decimal and hexadecimal. It also validates that unescaping properly
handles the case where unescaping an & produces a newly formed escape sequence (this should not be re-unescaped).

Servers may produce different output, this test is designed different unescapes clients must handle
Serializes string with escaping
Serializes simple scalar properties with xml preamble, comments and CDATA
Serializes string containing white space

response.body:
<?xml version = "1.0" encoding = "UTF-8"?>
<SimpleScalarPropertiesResponse>
    <stringValue> string with white    space </stringValue>
</SimpleScalarPropertiesResponse>

cases.description: Deserializes XML attributes on a payload when the xmlAttribute trait targets a member in the middle of the member list

given.name: XmlAttributesInMiddle
given.documentation: This example serializes an XML attribute on a payload when it's defined in the middle of the member list. This tests that implementations correctly write attributes immediately after the element start tag, which is critical for languages like C# where attribute writing must happen before child elements.

cases.description: Tests for XML list serialization

given.name: XmlLists
given.documentation:
This test case serializes XML lists for the following cases for both input and output:

  1. Normal XML lists.
  2. Normal XML sets.
  3. XML lists of lists.
  4. XML lists with @xmlName on its members
  5. Flattened XML lists.
  6. Flattened XML lists with @xmlName.
  7. Flattened XML lists with @xmlNamespace.
  8. Lists of structures.
  9. Flattened XML list of structures

cases.description: Serializes XML namespaces

response.body:
<XmlNamespacesResponse xmlns="http://foo.com">
    <nested xmlns="http://foo.com">
        <foo xmlns:baz="http://baz.com">Foo</foo>
        <values xmlns="http://qux.com">
            <member xmlns="http://bux.com">Bar</member>
            <member xmlns="http://bux.com">Baz</member>
        </values>
    </nested>
</XmlNamespacesResponse>
